title: RuleSentry v3 Schema Version Manifest
description: Version manifest for the RuleSentry v3 schema set, including release metadata, component versions, compatibility requirements, and deprecation notices.

schema_version.description: Semantic version of the v3 schema set (e.g., '3.0.0')

schema_release_date.description: ISO 8601 date when this schema version was released (e.g., '2025-11-19')

components.description: Component-level versioning for schema modules (core, api, etc.)

core.description: Version of all core schemas (base, rule, policy, category, extension, enforcement-response, etc.)

breaking_changes_since.description: Last schema version that was backward-compatible with this version. If this field is set to '2.9.0' and schema_version is '3.0.0', it means breaking changes were introduced in 3.0.0.

min_engine_version.description: Minimum RuleSentry engine version required to use this schema version

deprecations.description: List of deprecated features with sunset timelines

feature.description: Name or path of the deprecated feature (e.g., 'core/rule.schema.json#/properties/legacy_field')

deprecated_in.description: Schema version when this feature was deprecated

sunset_date.description: ISO 8601 date when this feature will be removed

reason.description: Explanation of why this feature is being deprecated

deprecations.reason: The 'namespace' field was redundant with 'name'. The 'name' field provides cleaner semantics and shorter syntax for extension identifiers.

deprecations.replacement: Use type='policy' with rule_categories for multi-category compliance bundles
deprecations.reason: Pack was a separate type for multi-category bundles, but policy with rule_categories provides the same functionality without a separate type discriminator.

deprecations.replacement: Use 'replace' with a descriptive replacement string, or 'redact' with a semantic marker
deprecations.reason: Placeholder, generalize, relativize, and pseudonymize transforms were removed to simplify the transform model. The 5 remaining types (mask, hash, replace, redact, tokenize) cover production use cases.
